macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $79,000 on ETF Inflows and Short Squeeze
items.summary: Bitcoin reached an 11-week high above $79,000 amid $1.5 billion in ETF inflows, $330 million in liquidations, and momentum from a reported Iran ceasefire extension by Trump. The price is consolidating around $77,000-$79,000 with eyes on $80,000 resistance.

items.title: Bitcoin Rally Liquidates $200M in Short Positions
items.summary: Bitcoin's sharp rally to $79,000 caught shorts offside, leading to over $200 million in liquidations and contributing to the price surge near multi-month highs.

items.title: Arbitrum Security Council Freezes $71M ETH Linked to Kelp Exploit
items.summary: Arbitrum froze 30,766 ETH (worth $71 million) tied to the Kelp exploit, recovering about a quarter of stolen funds via a governance-controlled wallet amid rising DeFi security concerns.

items.title: Circle Faces Class Action Lawsuit Over Hack Response
items.summary: Circle is sued for failing to freeze tokens during a hack without a court order, highlighting stablecoin security risks as wet markets also freeze assets post-exploits.

items.title: Crypto Market Cap Dips 0.8% to $2.6T as Altcoins Face Pressure
items.summary: The total crypto market capitalization fell 0.8% to $2.6 trillion in the past 24 hours, with altcoins under pressure while Bitcoin holds firm near recent highs.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: OpenAI Releases GPT Image 2.5 with Massive Leap in Photorealism
items.summary: GPT Image 2.5 achieves the biggest jump in AI image generation in over a year, featuring perfect photorealistic human faces, advanced lighting, accurate text in images, and flawless screenshots. It scores a +300 Elo jump on LLM Arena, enabling production-grade ads and fake dashboards from text prompts.

items.title: Seedance 2.0 Launches as Powerful AI Video Generation Tool
items.summary: Higgsfield releases Seedance 2.0, hailed as the most powerful video generation model, supporting one-prompt product ads with multi-angles, camera movement, consistent characters, and integration into real-world videos. Workflows demonstrated for turning blockouts into final renders using ComfyUI.

items.title: AI Agent Operates Real San Francisco Storefront and Hires Employees
items.summary: An AI agent is running a physical storefront in San Francisco, managing operations and hiring human employees, marking a practical application of AI in retail.

items.title: Abundly Demos Human + AI-Agent Development Team for Daily Releases
items.summary: Abundly showcases an AI agent system with Cursor for coding, Backlogger for tickets, Releaser for deployments, and Grace for stakeholder coordination, enabling daily platform releases alongside human engineers.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Quantum Motion Deploys First Full-Stack Silicon Quantum Computer
items.summary: Quantum Motion has achieved a milestone by deploying the first full-stack quantum computer using standard silicon chip fabrication processes. This development could accelerate the path to scalable quantum computing systems.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm Push Android PCs into Mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are advancing Android PCs to challenge Microsoft and Apple in personal computing. The initiative aims to scale the mobile OS to desktops for a unified computing experience.

items.title: TSMC Delays Use of ASML's High-NA EUV Machines Until 2030
items.summary: TSMC announced it will hold off on deploying ASML's advanced high-NA EUV lithography machines, costing over €350M each, for chip production through 2029 to reduce costs.

items.title: Tesla Plans to Use Intel's 14A Process for Terafab Chip Project
items.summary: Elon Musk stated Tesla will utilize Intel's next-generation 14A manufacturing process for chips at its Terafab project, positioning Tesla as Intel's first major customer for this technology.

items.title: Cloudsmith Raises $72M in Series C Funding
items.summary: Cloudsmith, a cloud-native platform for managing software artifacts, secured $72 million in Series C funding after a $23M Series B in 2025. The funding supports its software component protection services.

items.title: Deutsche Telekom Explores Merger with T-Mobile US
items.summary: Deutsche Telekom is seeking a potential combination with T-Mobile US amid global telecom sector consolidation. This follows discussions of major telecom mergers and financial stresses in the industry.
